score_evidence: The first transport render was rejected because the route cues read as heavy rods and the close-up did not prove that cargo crossed through a visible pore.
The improved close-up now frames a nuclear pore complex directly, with protein import cargo and small solute dots visible at the selective gate.
The generated scene uses the pore, chromatin, and nucleolus baseline images in the same Blender review board because no dedicated transport baseline PNG exists yet.
Cargo categories are visually distinct: green NLS protein import, pink mRNA export squiggles, paired magenta/cyan ribosomal subunit export, and subtle lilac solute dots.
The phase adds only Transport_Cargo and does not introduce rough ER, mitosis fragments, interaction hotspots, condensed chromosomes, or DNA export.
minor_issues: Some path guides still read slightly schematic compared with the organic baseline anatomy.
The green protein cargo is lumpy and tagged, but close-up silhouettes remain somewhat faceted and need final mesh smoothing or baked detail.
The reference board uses related nucleus baselines rather than a dedicated transport baseline image.
required_fixes: Before final presentation, add or source a dedicated nucleocytoplasmic transport baseline image for review scoring.
In a final polish pass, convert the path guides into subtler particle trails or Roblox Beam/Attachment paths so they do not dominate the anatomy.
Smooth or remesh protein cargo tokens before Roblox export.
